steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must explain why a constrained study like one finding the average age at which people first become parents is “much less dependent on high levels of funding.” The contrast word But signals this explanation will differ from the BHS situation, where funding is needed to track trends over long timescales.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — such studies are not trying to identify trends over time.: This directly explains the funding gap — the BHS needs funding to collect data across long timescales, but a study seeking a single snapshot statistic does not. ✓
  • Choice B — expanding the scope of such studies is unlikely to be feasible.: Feasibility of expansion is never raised in the passage; this doesn’t explain the funding difference. ✗
  • Choice C — the sociology study is unlikely to be able to recruit 10,000 participants.: Participant count is a detail about BHS, not a reason constrained studies need less funding. ✗
  • Choice D — such studies are likely to be a lower priority for funding than BHS.: This reverses the logic — the passage says they are less dependent on funding, not that they receive less of it. ✗
That matches Choice A — such studies are not trying to identify trends over time..
